name: android_pentest
description: Complete Android penetration testing suite with device reconnaissance, app analysis, vulnerability scanning, exploitation, network capture, and forensics
author: SecVulnHub Team | 0xb0rn3

optional_dependencies.aapt: For APK analysis - apt install aapt
optional_dependencies.apktool: For APK decompilation - apt install apktool
optional_dependencies.jadx: For DEX to Java decompilation - apt install jadx
optional_dependencies.keytool: For certificate analysis - included with JDK
optional_dependencies.frida: For runtime instrumentation - pip3 install frida-tools
optional_dependencies.objection: For Frida automation - pip3 install objection
optional_dependencies.requests: For network operations - pip3 install requests
optional_dependencies.cryptography: For certificate parsing - pip3 install cryptography

help.description: Android penetration testing framework covering device fingerprinting, static/dynamic app analysis, vulnerability scanning, exploitation testing, network traffic interception, and forensics. Detects 50+ vulnerability types including SQL injection, insecure storage, cleartext traffic, exported components, and more. Includes automated exploitation chains for common Android vulnerabilities.

examples: recon          — Device fingerprint, root/bootloader/SELinux/patch-level
app_scan       — Static APK analysis, manifest, permissions, secret scan
vuln_scan      — Device + app CVE assessment
exploit        — Intent injection, SQLi on content providers, component abuse
network        — tcpdump capture + logcat credential leakage
forensics      — DB/SharedPreferences extraction, logcat, ADB backup
full           — Runs recon → app_scan → vuln_scan → exploit → network → forensics
frida_hook     — Deploy frida-server, SSL unpin, root bypass, dump creds, trace
objection_patch — Embed Frida gadget via Objection (no root at runtime)
backdoor_apk   — Pull APK, inject msfvenom payload (-x template), sign, reinstall
rebuild        — Rebuild boot persistence APK via build_bootbuddy.py (proven WAN C2)
deploy_shell   — Generate fresh msfvenom APK and install via adb
adb_wifi       — Enable ADB over TCP/WiFi (adb tcpip 5555)
get_root       — Multi-vector root: Magisk/su, adb root, CVE-2024-0044, mtk-su, KernelSU
exploit_cve    — Targeted CVE: CVE-2024-0044, CVE-2023-45866, CVE-2024-31317
cve_chain      — Chain multiple CVE exploits for privilege escalation
zero_click     — Zero-interaction vectors: NFC, BLE, WiFi
persist        — Boot Receiver persistence (no root), Magisk post-fs-data.d / service module
hook           — secV hook layer: monitor calls + C2 relay
unhook         — Remove secV hooks cleanly
device_net_scan — Scan device WiFi IP via netrecon
msf_handler    — Start msfrpcd Metasploit handler
wan_expose     — Expose MSF + APK server via Cloudflare Tunnel
qr_exploit     — QR code payload: APK URL, Intent URI, ADB wireless pairing, deeplink
inject_agent   — Push ARM64/shell agent, receive JSON recon + C2 callback
full_pwn       — Auto-chain: recon → adb_wifi → get_root → device_net_scan → deploy_shell → persist → wan_expose
multi_device   — Run any sub-operation on ALL connected devices simultaneously

device.description: Android device serial number (auto-detected if not specified)
examples: emulator-5554
device_serial_number

package.description: Target package name for app-specific operations
examples: com.example.app
com.target.vulnerable

proxy_host.description: Proxy server IP (auto-detected if not specified)

deep_analysis.description: Use jadx Java decompilation for deeper code analysis (slower)

cleanup.description: Delete work directory after operation completes

third_party_only.description: Only scan user-installed apps (skip system apps)

scan_limit.description: Max apps to scan when no package is specified

nvd_api_key.description: NVD API key for real-time CVE lookups (also reads NVD_API_KEY env var)

lhost.description: Attacker IP or hostname for reverse shells and payload delivery (auto-detected from default route)
examples: attacker.example.com

lport.description: Listener port for reverse shells and MSF handler

payload.description: Metasploit payload for backdoor_apk / deploy_shell / msf_handler. Shorthand: tcp, http, https, shell, stageless.

install.description: After backdoor_apk or deploy_shell: uninstall original and install backdoored APK on device

hook_mode.description: Frida hook mode for frida_hook operation

hook_timeout.description: Seconds to keep Frida attached before detaching (frida_hook)

trace_method.description: Class or method substring to trace (used with hook_mode=trace)

c2_host.description: Attacker IP for agent callback in inject_agent (auto-detected)

c2_port.description: TCP port for agent C2 listener (inject_agent)

c2_timeout.description: Seconds to wait for agent TCP callback (inject_agent)

escalate.description: Automatically issue ROOT_SHELL or SHELL when agent connects in exploit/c2 mode

qr_mode.description: QR code payload type for qr_exploit

pair_port.description: Wireless ADB pairing port for qr_exploit mode=adb_pair (Android 11+)

pair_code.description: Wireless ADB pairing code for qr_exploit mode=adb_pair

cve.description: CVE identifier for exploit_cve operation (e.g. CVE-2024-0044)

adb_port.description: TCP port to use when enabling ADB over WiFi (adb_wifi operation)

serve_port.description: HTTP server port for APK delivery (wan_expose / qr_exploit)

apk_path.description: Path to original boot persistence APK for rebuild operation (auto-pulled from device if omitted)

out_path.description: Output APK path for rebuild operation (default: apk_backdoor/output/rebuilt.apk)

msf.description: Merge msfvenom meterpreter into APK during rebuild (WAN C2 via bore tunnels)

msf_lport.description: Meterpreter callback port for rebuild --msf mode

examples.description: Boot persistence (no root via Boot Receiver)
Rebuild boot persistence APK with WAN meterpreter (proven)
Run recon on all connected devices

features: SQL injection testing in Content Providers

recon.description: Device reconnaissance and security posture assessment
checks: Build ID and security patch level

app_scan.output: Detailed application security profile with risk assessment

vuln_scan.output: Prioritized vulnerability report with remediation advice

exploit.note: All tests are non-destructive and ethical

network.output: Network traffic report with security findings

forensics.output: Forensics artifacts and sensitive data report

hardcoded_secrets.description: Hardcoded passwords, API keys, or tokens
hardcoded_secrets.remediation: Store secrets in Android Keystore, use environment variables

insecure_crypto.description: Weak cryptographic algorithms (DES, MD5, SHA1, ECB)
insecure_crypto.remediation: Use AES-256, SHA-256+, secure cipher modes

webview_vulns.remediation: Disable JavaScript when not needed, validate URLs

intent_hijacking.remediation: Validate all intent data, use explicit intents

outdated_sdk.remediation: Update to latest Android API level

root_detection.impact: All security controls can be bypassed

installation_tiers.basic: Device recon and basic app analysis (stdlib + adb)
installation_tiers.standard: Static APK analysis — add aapt, keytool (included with JDK)
installation_tiers.runtime: Frida instrumentation — pip3 install frida-tools objection

notes: ⚠️  AUTHORIZATION REQUIRED - Only test devices you own or have permission to test
⚠️  Some operations require root access (network capture, forensics)
⚠️  Exploitation testing is non-destructive and ethical only
⚠️  Respect privacy laws and data protection regulations
💡 Start with 'recon' operation to understand device security posture
💡 Use 'app_scan' for comprehensive application security analysis
💡 'network' operation requires root for packet capture
💡 'forensics' extracts maximum data with root access
💡 Connect device via USB or ADB over network
💡 Enable USB debugging in Developer Options
💡 Accept ADB authorization prompt on device
💡 Results saved to temporary directory with full details
💡 Combine with other SecV modules for complete assessment
help.legal_disclaimer: This tool is designed exclusively for authorized security testing and research. Users must obtain explicit written permission before testing any Android devices or applications they do not own. Unauthorized access to mobile devices may violate computer fraud and abuse laws in your jurisdiction. The authors and contributors assume no liability for misuse. Always follow ethical hacking guidelines and responsible disclosure practices.
